title: Devastating toxic spill seen as test of whether African countries will stand up to China - BusinessGhana
impact_evidence: Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Tailings generation and management -> "The tailings dam - used to store toxic by-products from the copper mining process, including heavy metals like arsenic, mercury and lead - had collapsed into a tributary connected to the Kafue, Zambia's longest river and a major drinking water source." | "At least 50,000 tonnes of acidic debris spilled out into the surrounding waterways and farmland, according to the government." || Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "The spill has killed the fish around the towns of Chambishi and Kitwe, made the water undrinkable and destroyed crops, farmers have told the BBC." | "For two weeks after the spill, Lamec says he and his family weren't allowed to use the local water until it had been sprayed with lime to help reduce the acidity." || Environmental | Water Resources | Freshwater ecotoxicity (heavy metals, pH changes, sensitive species) -> "The tailings dam - used to store toxic by-products from the copper mining process, including heavy metals like arsenic, mercury and lead - had collapsed ..." | "Toxic metals that can cause kidney damage and cancers, as well as gastric and intestinal issues, could be carried downstream to the capital, Lusaka, says Dr Mweene Himwiinga, a senior lecturer at Zambia's Copperbelt University." || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Topsoil degradation and loss of soil fertility -> "In the village of Twalima, it has been a struggle to grow anything since the spill, say farmers Abigail Namtowe, 28, and Frederick Bwalya, 72." | "Mr Bwalya has lived in Twalima for 33 years. ... "Farming is not going well because the soil is not clean," he says." || Environmental | Biodiversity & Ecosystems | Impacts on aquatic ecosystems and life below water -> "The spill has killed the fish around the towns of Chambishi and Kitwe, made the water undrinkable and destroyed crops, farmers have told the BBC." | "Authorities have imposed a fishing ban on the Kafue River after the toxic spill" || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Mental and physical health impacts on communities -> "Toxic metals that can cause kidney damage and cancers, as well as gastric and intestinal issues, could be carried downstream to the capital, Lusaka, says Dr Mweene Himwiinga..." | "He also describes a severe pain in his leg which won't go away and makes it difficult for him to move. "The doctor says the contaminated water could be causing this," he adds." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "Farming was the main source of food and income for Ms Namtowe's family, and her six year-old daughter is now very hungry and malnourished, she told us." | "The spill has ... destroyed crops, farmers have told the BBC." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "The mine is owned by Sino-Metals Leach Zambia, a subsidiary of a Chinese state-owned firm, which has created - according to Chinese sources - more than 2,000 jobs." | "The Chinese embassy in Zambia says Sino Metals has cooperated fully with the investigation into the incident, that compensation has been paid to 454 households and that all staff at the site have continued to be employed and receive their salaries, despite a six-month pause in mining." || Governance | Risk Management & Long-Term Stewardship | Tailings and waste governance -> "The tailings dam - used to store toxic by-products ... - had collapsed into a tributary connected to the Kafue..." | "They say the dam's collapse was caused by a number of factors, including engineering failures, construction flaws and operational mismanagement." | "The clean-up has been delayed by issues between the consultants hired to assess the damage and Sino Metals, Dr Chibamba told the BBC..." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Accountability for accidents and environmental damage -> "In September, 176 farmers filed an $80bn (£58bn) lawsuit against Sino Metals and NFC Africa, the Chinese firm that owns the land the mine is on." | "The Chinese embassy in Lusaka disputes the scale of the damage and told the BBC it welcomed the establishment of an independent investigation into the incident."
